title: Small](https://www.kaggle.com/code/oskarseweryn/playground-s6e5-tabular-ensemble/log%22,%22https://www.kaggle.com/code/mohankrishnathalla/ps-s6e5-full-eda-feature-engineering-models%22]},{%22title%22:%22Small) OOF stacking layer for model diversity
summary: Use out-of-fold predictions from a few diverse base models as inputs to a lightweight logistic or ridge-style stacker, with a simple average as the control.
rationale: External exact-competition notebooks emphasize stacking from strong OOF predictions. Different models may rank boundary pit-window cases differently even with the same features.
implementation_hint: Generate OOF predictions from direct tree models and optionally one neural tabular model; train a regularized logistic regression or shallow LightGBM stacker only on OOF predictions and a few core context features. Compare stacker versus equal-weight and OOF-AUC-weighted blends.
expected_effect: Small to medium AUC gain with medium confidence if base predictions are sufficiently diverse.
risk: Stacking is highly validation-sensitive and can overfit when folds leak Race-Year context. Keep the stacker low-capacity and validate with grouped folds.
